description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular profile and a coarse, pitted surface. Its coloration is predominantly dark gray, interspersed with lighter gray and white mineral inclusions, giving it a speckled appearance. The rock's surface texture varies from rough and jagged on one side to slightly smoother but still uneven on the other, with visible cavities and protrusions. The edges are sharp and fractured, suggesting it may have broken off from a larger formation. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible internal voids or cracks that penetrate deeply. Its overall form and weathered look suggest it could be found in an urban park or used as a naturalistic barrier in landscaping.
description: A dark gray, rugged rock with a coarse, pitted surface and scattered white mineral speckles. Its irregular, angular shape and rough texture make it visually striking and naturalistic.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rock's jagged, angular edge and deep surface pits, emphasizing its roughest and most irregular side.
description_view_1: This side shows a broader, flatter face with prominent white and light gray speckles, giving a clearer view of the rock's mineral composition.
description_view_2: Here, the rock's angular top and a mix of rough and smoother surfaces are visible, with several shallow cavities and a pronounced edge.
description_view_3: This perspective reveals the darkest, most shadowed side, with deep recesses and a more blocky, less speckled appearance.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
